finding.summary: Levels, headroom, and dynamics are healthy: sample peak -0.8 dB, true peak -0.4 dB, RMS -18 dB, crest factor 12 dB, integrated LUFS -20, LRA 8 LU, silence fraction only 0.05 (so stats are not diluted by dead air). The single observable irregularity is a strongly low-tilted 1/3-octave spectrum: 100 Hz at -18 dB, 1 kHz at -24 dB, 4 kHz at -30 dB — about 6 dB drop per decade. For an element labelled 'Pluck Synth' this is unusually bottom-heavy; a lead pluck typically carries more energy in the 1–4 kHz clarity/definition band. Stereo data is null, so width and correlation cannot be assessed.
finding.probable_cause: Most likely the synth patch itself is bass-heavy (a sub layer, low fundamental, or marimba-style voicing). The track's only FX is a VST3 'Generic Delay' with an empty parameters array, so I cannot determine whether the delay is adding low-end smear, contributing at all, or set to a tempo-synced value. With only three 1/3-octave bands across four decades of frequency, I cannot locate the actual tilt onset (it could be a narrow resonance near 100 Hz or a broadband shelf).
finding.confidence_reason: The spectrum payload contains only three bands (100 Hz, 1 kHz, 4 kHz) with no data between them — a 12 dB potential issue between 100 Hz and 1 kHz, and another between 1 kHz and 4 kHz, cannot be localized. Stereo capture is null. The delay exposes no parameters. 'Pluck Synth' is ambiguous about intended tonal balance and could legitimately be a bass/marimba-style patch. Without an EQ in the chain and without visible delay settings, no targeted intervention is verifiable from the data.

evidence_refs.description: 12 dB, moderate dynamics appropriate for plucked material
0.05 — stats are not diluted by silence, confidence in levels is high
3 bands: 100 Hz -18 dB, 1 kHz -24 dB, 4 kHz -30 dB; low-frequency tilt of ~6 dB/decade
1/3-octave band levels, power-domain L+R sum, no phase cancellation
VST3: Generic Delay — only FX in chain
Empty array — no parameters exposed, delay settings unverifiable

proposal.reason: No single safe move is supported by the evidence. Track volume, pan, headroom, crest factor, and integrated loudness are all within healthy ranges and do not justify a gain change. The only FX in the chain is a delay with no exposed parameters, so a set_fx_param change would be a guess; a set_fx_bypass preview would risk removing an intentional effect whose contribution I cannot measure. There is no EQ in the chain to address the spectral tilt, and even if there were, I am not confident the tilt is a defect rather than the intended patch voicing given only three spectrum bands and a null stereo capture. The most honest output is to flag the observation and request a denser spectrum capture and/or exposed delay parameters before any parameter change.
